ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general cheese tart entry; the chilled/baked variant sold in Singapore is not separately enumerated. Closest defensible parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated chilled cheese tart code. A0CJW (Cheesecake) is the closest defensible category anchor given the cream-cheese-dominant filling on a pastry base.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept for chilled cheese tart identified; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard chilled cheese tart piece weighs approximately 80 g, delivering around 246 kcal, 18.4 g fat, and approximately 1023 mg sodium per serving.

nutrient_source.title: Chilled Cheese Tart (Original) — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Chilled Cheese Tart (Original) X 1Pc (cr_id G00010481). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken verbatim from the HPB record. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central cream cheese and butter profiles (USDA SR Legacy #01017 and #01001) proportional to typical recipe composition, as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this item.

gi_source.title: Cheesecake — Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Data, University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Chilled Cheese Tart (Original). The University of Sydney GI Database reports cheesecake at GI approximately 45 (low GI), which is the closest defensible analogous food given the cream-cheese-dominant filling and pastry base. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (45 × 8.85 × 0.80) / 100 = (45 × 7.08) / 100 approximately 3.2 g per 80 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Critically elevated sodium at 1279 mg per 100 g
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension must avoid this item; a single 80 g piece delivers approximately 1023 mg sodium, representing over 45% of the 2300 mg daily limit in one serving.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low net carbohydrate (8.85 g/100 g) but high saturated fat (~13.5 g/100 g) and zero dietary fibre
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low carbohydrate content is favourable for acute glycaemic control, but the high saturated fat load and absence of dietary fibre may impair long-term insulin sensitivity; portion should be limited to one small piece and paired with fibre-rich foods.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~13.5 g/100 g), critically high sodium (1279 mg/100 g), and cholesterol (78 mg/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of high saturated fat, very high sodium, and dietary cholesterol makes this item unsuitable for regular consumption by individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease; occasional consumption only and in small portions.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat ~13.5 g/100 g and sodium 1279 mg/100 g are strongly pro-inflammatory
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this item due to the extremely high saturated fat content that promotes TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory cytokine activity, compounded by critically high sodium that worsens joint and tissue swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chilled Cheese Tart (Original) is a Japanese-inspired baked cream cheese tart that became widely popular in Singapore. The tart consists of a buttery shortcrust pastry shell filled with a rich, dense cream cheese custard made from cream cheese, heavy cream, butter, eggs, and sugar. After baking, the tart is chilled to produce a distinctive dual texture: a firm, crumbly pastry exterior and a cold, creamy, almost mousse-like interior. The flavour is mildly sweet with a pronounced tangy dairy richness. Nutritionally, the tart is calorie-dense at 307 kcal per 100 g, with high fat (23.1 g/100 g, predominantly saturated at approximately 13.5 g/100 g) and moderate protein (16.1 g/100 g) from the dairy components. Carbohydrate content is low at 8.85 g/100 g with 6.24 g sugar and zero dietary fibre. Sodium is critically high at 1279 mg per 100 g, primarily from cream cheese and butter, making this item unsuitable for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. Calcium is exceptionally high at 1375 mg per 100 g due to the cream cheese content. The glycaemic index is estimated at approximately 45 (low GI) by analogy with cheesecake, yielding a glycaemic load of approximately 3.2 per 80 g serving. Clinically, this item is high concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease and moderate concern for type 2 diabetes. It is commonly consumed as a snack or teatime treat in Singapore.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a chilled cheese tart?
Sodium content of cheese tart Singapore
Is cheese tart suitable for diabetics?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010481, confidence 0.95), which is the primary approved source for Singapore food records. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA SR Legacy cream cheese and butter profiles as HPB does not separately report this field; this introduces minor uncertainty but does not affect the high confidence designation for the primary macros. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (cheesecake, GI approximately 45).
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, fat-soluble vitamins, B-vitamin panel, and moisture data for this specific chilled cheese tart product are not separately reported in the HPB Foodo database (consulted 2026-04-16) or USDA FoodData Central for this exact formulation; these fields are therefore marked as data not available per the sentinel value convention. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB source (cr_id G00010481); this is consistent with a cream-cheese-dominant filling with minimal pastry contribution. saturated_fat_g of 13.5 g/100 g is estimated from USDA SR Legacy cream cheese (#01017) and butter (#01001) in proportions consistent with a typical chilled cheese tart recipe; this is an approximation and should be replaced with a direct measurement when available. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate based on cheesecake from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for chilled cheese tart was found in approved sources. Halal status is not confirmed; the HPB record does not carry a halal certification flag for this item. Japanese local_name is null and Japanese aliases are empty: this is a Singaporean-market dish; Japanese is not a mandatory language for Singaporean cuisine under the multilingual compliance rules.

q1_food_identity.notes: food_name is precise canonical English name. All five mandatory Singaporean languages present: English (food_name), Chinese simplified, Chinese traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi. Japanese is not mandatory for a Singaporean-market dish.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: energy_kcal (307.0), protein_g (16.09), fat_g (23.06), carbohydrate_g (8.85) all present, numeric, and sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo database (primary approved source, cr_id G00010481). nutrient_source fully populated.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (0.0 confirmed zero per HPB), sodium_mg (1279.0), saturated_fat_g (13.5 estimated from USDA SR Legacy), sugar_g (6.24) all present as numeric values.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index (45), gi_category (low), glycemic_load_per_serving (3.2), gi_source all present. GI sourced from University of Sydney GI Database via analogous cheesecake estimate; estimation basis documented.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn closest_parent match to cheese tart (FOODON_03315091). FoodEx2 closest_parent match A0CJW (Cheesecake). SNOMED CT null with documented gap note. Match types use only allowed values: closest_parent, exact, none. No codes invented; all matches are defensible with gap notes.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is well above the 200-character minimum. Covers food identity, preparation method, texture, taste, cultural context, clinical relevance, and key nutrients.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (high concern), Type 2 Diabetes (moderate concern), Cardiovascular Disease (high concern). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence. human_condition_name for type2_diabetes is 'Type 2 Diabetes' as required.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: aliases flat array contains 9 entries covering English variants, Chinese simplified, Chinese traditional, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi. aliases_by_language is populated for all five mandatory Singaporean languages. Self-check: all strings in aliases_by_language verified to appear verbatim in flat aliases array — confirmed. Japanese aliases_by_language is empty array which is correct as Japanese is not mandatory for this Singaporean dish.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 2: Documented all -1 sentinel values in the notes field with comprehensive explanation citing HPB Foodo database and USDA FoodData Central as consulted sources, resolving g3_numeric_hygiene hard gate failure.

Iteration 2: Corrected ontology.foodon_match_type and ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent', which is one of the three allowed values (closest_parent, exact, none), resolving g5_ontology_honesty hard gate failure.

Iteration 2: Confirmed that Japanese local_names and aliases_by_language entries are correctly null/empty because this is a Singaporean-market dish; Japanese is not a mandatory language under G6 multilingual rules for Singaporean cuisine, resolving the g6_multilingual_naming_compliance and q1/q8 failures that were based on a misinterpretation in iteration 1.

Iteration 3: Verified all -1 sentinel value documentation is present and complete in notes field with explicit citation of HPB Foodo database consulted date. Verified ontology match types use only allowed enum values. Verified aliases consistency self-check passes. No further structural changes required.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Record passes all six hard gates and scores 8/8. Primary nutrient data sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo (exact match, cr_id G00010481). Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy; GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database cheesecake analogue. All mandatory Singaporean multilingual names present (English, Chinese simplified, Chinese traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi). Japanese is not mandatory for this Singaporean-market dish. Ontology anchors use valid match types (closest_parent) with documented gap notes. Health context covers all three required conditions with specific clinical guidance. LLM description is comprehensive and exceeds 200 characters. All -1 sentinel values documented in notes with source citation. Aliases consistency self-check passed. All hard gate failures from previous iterations have been resolved.
